interactiveVocabulary.definition: to make someone feel better when they are sad or having a difficult time.
examples: They help each other get through hard times.

interactiveVocabulary.definition: to speak about something briefly or refer to it.
examples: Do not mention one friend's faults to another.

interactiveVocabulary.definition: a temporary state of mind or feeling.
examples: Friends remain friends even when one is in a bad mood, or emotional state.

interactiveVocabulary.definition: in a correct or suitable way.
examples: Friends often greet each other properly.
A handshake is the correct way.

interactiveVocabulary.definition: plans or means to do something.
examples: This girl intends to keep in touch with a friend.
She plans to send e-mail every day.

interactiveVocabulary.definition: was made up of or composed of certain things.
examples: This lively day consisted, or was made up of, bike riding and fresh air.

interactiveVocabulary.definition: feeling certain or confident about something.
examples: These friends are positive that they're having a good time.
With his advanced chess skills, this man can teach his young friend to play.
Friends may act in peculiar, or unusual, ways when taking photos.

interactiveVocabulary.definition: a natural ability to do something well.
examples: These friends share a talent for music.
This ability gives them hours of fun.
